quick_skim.title: Transformation Behavior of Images in Latent Space
quick_skim.abstract: Training of neural networks for histopathology classification tasks typically relies on data encoding into latent space, which reduces complexity and improves performance. There are several encoder networks available, either pretrained on general image datasets such as ImageNET, or specifically on histopathological images. Training of encoder networks should be adapted to downstream tasks, allowing encoding of biologic/diagnostic content while rendering networks invariant to label-irrelevant transformations.   This paper investigates the effect of classical image transformation on the latent space, using networks provided by Lunit Inc. and Bioptimus, both focusing on pathological images, and by Meta Research Team. We assess variance of embeddings resulting from standard data transformations by comparing original and transformed image embeddings and by contrasting them with random, unrelated embeddings, using image tiles from hematoxylin/eosin-stained sections available in a colorectal tissue dataset and the publicly accessible TCGA dataset.   Our findings show that embeddings of original and transformed images are closer to each other than to random embeddings, indicating robustness to transformations. However, they are not fully invariant, revealing that the encoder networks do not completely neutralize transformation effects in latent space, explaining why transformation-mediated augmentation of datasets can improve performance. Significant differences were observed between general and histopathology-specific encoder networks.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Investigates histopathology-specific pretrained encoders and their latent space embeddings under image transformations
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 研究病理组织学预训练编码器在图像变换下的潜在空间嵌入
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 研究病理组织学预训练编码器在图像变换下的潜在空间嵌入
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 研究病理组织学预训练编码器在图像变换下的潜在空间嵌入
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper evaluates how image transformations affect latent embeddings from pretrained histopathology encoders, providing insights into patch-level representation robustness for downstream tasks.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对病理图像分类中编码器对无关变换不变性的需求，系统评估了Lunit、Bioptimus等组织病理学专用预训练网络在标准图像变换下潜在空间嵌入的方差。实验揭示了不同编码器对旋转变换等操作的敏感性差异，为构建稳健的图块级表示提供了选择依据，有助于提升数字病理下游任务的特征提取质量。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文针对病理图像分类中编码器对无关变换不变性的需求，系统评估了Lunit、Bioptimus等组织病理学专用预训练网络在标准图像变换下潜在空间嵌入的方差。实验揭示了不同编码器对旋转变换等操作的敏感性差异，为构建稳健的图块级表示提供了选择依据，有助于提升数字病理下游任务的特征提取质量。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: techniques for extracting patch-level representations from pathological models
